output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 4/4.
Batch 1: Batch 1 executed. T1 and T2 are complete in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts`, the checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/multi-clip-drag-should-20260406-0421/execution_batch_1.json`, and verification passed with `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts`, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`, and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`.
Batch 2: Batch 2 executed. Added `multi-drag-utils` unit coverage for the new helpers and augmented planning cases, wired the multi-clip new-track drop path in `useClipDrag.ts` to commit via `type: 'config'`, ran lint/type/module verification successfully, and wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/multi-clip-drag-should-20260406-0421/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Batch 3 executed as a verification-only step. No source changes were required: `computeDropPosition` already emits the same `isNewTrack` signal for multi-drag, `DropIndicator` already shows the new-track affordance when `trackId` is undefined, and secondary ghosts remain visible near edges by rendering relative to the source row when there is not yet a concrete target row. Verification passed and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/multi-clip-drag-should-20260406-0421/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Batch 4 executed. Added the missing integration coverage in `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, ran a temporary repro script for the exact multi-clip new-track scenario and deleted it, then ran the full `src/tools/video-editor/` regression successfully. The batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/multi-clip-drag-should-20260406-0421/execution_batch_4.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented `buildAugmentedData` in `multi-drag-utils.ts`. It uses `addTrack(data.resolvedConfig, kind, insertAtTop ? 0 : undefined)`, diffs augmented tracks against the original track IDs to identify the single new track, inserts an empty row at index 0 for top insertion or at the end for bottom insertion, and returns augmented TimelineData plus `newTrackId`.

Implemented `buildConfigFromDragResult` in `multi-drag-utils.ts`. It merges `metaUpdates` into `baseMeta` before any timing reads, derives clip positions from `nextRows`, and rebuilds resolved clips with `rowsToConfig`-equivalent hold/non-hold timing rules so updated `from`/`to` values from overlap resolution are serialized from merged metadata rather than stale base metadata.

Created `src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.test.ts` and verified the helper behavior at module level: top and bottom augmented track insertion, explicit null return when `addTrack` fails to create a new track, merged overlap-timing `metaUpdates` flowing into `buildConfigFromDragResult`, successful planning onto a new anchor track, and rejection when a secondary clip would need an additional new track.

Updated the multi-clip branch in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` so cross-track drops with `dropPosition.isNewTrack` augment the timeline first, plan moves against the augmented rows, build a final resolved config through `buildConfigFromDragResult`, and commit through `applyEdit({ type: 'config', resolvedConfig })`. Verified that the existing rows-based fallback remains for `!isNewTrack`, augmentation failure, and `canMove:false` cases.

Verified the near-edge multi-drag behavior without a code patch. `computeDropPosition` derives `isNewTrack` from row geometry and source kind rather than selection size, so multi-clip drags get the same new-track state as single-clip drags. `DropIndicator` already shows the affordance when `position.isNewTrack === true && position.trackId === undefined`. `useClipDrag` still falls back to `session.sourceRowId` for secondary ghost placement when no real target row exists, and `computeSecondaryGhosts` therefore keeps secondary ghosts visible relative to that source row. This matches the plan’s acceptable FLAG-002 behavior, so the optional virtual-row polish was not needed.

Added an integration test in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx` for a multi-clip drop onto a new bottom track. The test drives pointerdown/move/up with `isNewTrack: true` and verifies `applyEdit` receives `type: 'config'` plus a resolved config containing the new track and moved clips. I also inspected `useClipDrag.ts` to confirm the single-clip new-track branch at lines 365-377 remains untouched and the existing-track multi-drag rows path still lives in the fallback at lines 419-440. Final verification passed with the full video-editor regression suite.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed `buildAugmentedData` finds the new track by diffing augmented vs original track IDs and inserts the empty row at index 0 when `insertAtTop` is true, otherwise at the end.
Confirmed `buildConfigFromDragResult` constructs merged metadata before reading `from`/`to`/`hold`, satisfying FLAG-001 so overlap-resolution patches are applied before serialization.

Confirmed by `multi-drag-utils.test.ts` that `buildConfigFromDragResult` consumes overlap-style `metaUpdates` with adjusted `from`/`to` values and emits those adjusted values for the affected clip instead of the stale base metadata, while an untouched clip remains unchanged.

Confirmed in `useClipDrag.ts` that the prior `!isNewTrack` multi-drag rows path is preserved inside the `!handledNewTrackMove` fallback, and the new `isNewTrack` branch only handles the drop after successful augmentation and `canMove:true` planning; otherwise control falls through to the existing behavior.

Confirmed by inspection that the DropIndicator affordance is driven by `isNewTrack && trackId === undefined`, which `computeDropPosition` already produces for multi-drag and `useDragCoordinator` already forwards unchanged. Confirmed that secondary ghosts remain visible near edges because `useClipDrag` falls back to `session.sourceRowId`, so `computeSecondaryGhosts` renders them against a real source row instead of disappearing.

Confirmed by the final full regression run that all existing video-editor tests passed after the change set: `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/` completed with 24 passed test files and 89 passed tests, including the single-clip drag path, same-track multi-drag, cross-track multi-drag to existing tracks, and the new multi-clip new-track integration case.
